title: Vendor runtime CCU/SMHC0 register read audit
question: Do vendor Linux runtime CCU, NSI, MBUS, or SMHC0 clock/reset registers expose an obvious disabled gate or secure switch difference that explains mainline SMHC0 IDMAC descriptor-read stalls?
test_cost: low; read-only /dev/mem register reads on Cubie2 and Cubie3 vendor Linux, no reboot and no kernel build

inputs.note: An initial read using the manual-style 0x02001000 base returned zeros. Vendor DTS proves the live vendor CCU base is 0x02002000.

decision.summary: Vendor Linux shows live nonzero CCU/NSI/MBUS/SMHC0 clock and reset registers at the vendor DTS base. SMHC0 clock and BGR are enabled on both Cubie2 and Cubie3, CCMU_SEC_SWITCH_REG reads zero, and no obvious disabled fabric gate appears. The earlier zero reads were caused by using the wrong CCU base, not by inaccessible registers.
decision.next_step: Do not patch from H074. Continue waiting for maintainer/list/vendor clues or a source-backed handoff/security detail. If runtime comparison continues, use 0x02002000 as the vendor CCU base.
